D5 Design L4 Analyze Reference

WCAG Ratio vs APCA

the old contrast ratio ignores which colour is on top — swap text and background and watch only APCA notice

WCAG 2.x scores a pair by a luminance ratio from 1:1 to 21:1, with 4.5:1 the body-text threshold. It is symmetric — swap text and background and nothing changes. APCA's Lc is signed: dark-on-light and light-on-dark score differently, matching how the eye actually reads. For text contrast, WCAG 2.1 and 2.2 are identical; 2.2 only added focus-indicator rules.

Set the two lightnesses, then hit swap. The WCAG ratio is unmoved; the APCA Lc flips and changes magnitude. That asymmetry is the gap WCAG 3 means to close.
